transcript:
HCP: hi there, whats bothering you?
Patient: uh so i got this rash on my arm yesterday and its really itchy
HCP: show me, whats it like?
Patient: looks like red bumps, mostly on my elbow and forearm
HCP: any recent bug bites or new creams?
Patient: i did get bitten by a mosquito a week ago but i havent used any new stuff
HCP: ok, any other symptoms like breathing issues or swelling?
Patient: no just the itching
HCP: let me have a look, yeah lots of papules and some excoriation
HCP: looks like insect bite hypersensitivity, keep the area cool and avoid scratching
HCP: i'll write you a prescription for calamine lotion
Patient: thanks doc, i'll pick that up

hpi.text: rash on arm for a day, red bumps on elbow and forearm, mosquito bite a week ago
hpi.evidence_span: uh so i got this rash on my arm yesterday and its really itchy

exam.text: red bumps on elbow and forearm, papules and excoriation
exam.evidence_span: looks like red bumps, mostly on my elbow and forearm

items.action: keep area cool and avoid scratching
items.evidence_span: keep the area cool and avoid scratching
i'll write you a prescription for calamine lotion

transcript:
HCP: hi there, good to see you, what brings you in today
Patient: yeah, so i've got this really bad ankle sprain, happened yesterday afternoon
HCP: oh right, how did that happen?
Patient: was playing footy with the mates, twisted my ankle pretty badly
HCP: okay, so it's swollen and hurts a lot now?
Patient: yeah, can't really put weight on it, throbbing pain
HCP: let me take a look, seems quite swollen and warm to touch
HCP: sounds like a grade two sprain, let's get you some ice and rest
Patient: yeah, definitely need some pain relief too
HCP: try some ibuprofen, maybe a little ice pack on it
Patient: thanks, i'll do that
HCP: if it doesn't settle in a couple of days, come back and we'll check it out

hpi.text: twisted ankle playing footy yesterday, swollen, throbbing pain, can't put weight on it
hpi.evidence_span: twisted my ankle pretty badly

exam.text: ankle swollen and warm to touch
exam.evidence_span: seems quite swollen and warm to touch

problems.name: grade two sprain
problems.evidence_span: sounds like a grade two sprain

items.evidence_span: get you some ice and rest
if it doesn't settle in a couple of days, come back

transcript:
HCP: hello, whats wrong today?
Patient: uh i've got a bit of a cough and i'm feeling really tired all the time
HCP: how long for?
Patient: like a week now, started with a runny nose
HCP: any wheezing or chest pain?
Patient: no, just the cough is getting worse at night
HCP: ok, so no asthma history?
Patient: no, never had asthma
HCP: what meds are you taking?
Patient: just a bit of ibuprofen for the headache sometimes
HCP: allergies?
Patient: uh no allergies that i know of
HCP: ok, i'll listen to your lungs
HCP: lungs sound clear, but your nose is blocked
HCP: sounds like a viral upper respiratory infection, rest, fluids, maybe some steam
HCP: and ibuprofen for pain if needed, come back if you get short of breath

transcript:
HCP: hi, welcome in, what's troubling you?
Patient: hello doctor, um i've got this terrible knee pain that's been going on for about two weeks
HCP: right, where exactly?
Patient: the right knee, mostly the front part, and it's really stiff in the morning
HCP: any swelling?
Patient: a bit, looks puffy compared to the left one
HCP: have you been doing a lot of walking or running?
Patient: yeah, started back at the gym last month, running on the treadmill
HCP: any history of knee problems?
Patient: not really, just a mild sprain years ago
HCP: and meds?
Patient: taking panadol for it sometimes, and i have hypertension so i'm on amlodipine
HCP: any allergies?
Patient: no, never had any
HCP: ok, let me have a look, bearing weight is painful and there is some effusion
HCP: knee feels warm to touch but no redness, range of motion is limited due to pain
HCP: sounds like a meniscal irritation or early osteoarthritis flare, let's get an ultrasound to be sure
HCP: i'll write you a script for meloxicam, continue your amlodipine, and refer you to physio for strengthening
HCP: come back if the swelling gets worse or you can't walk
Patient: thanks doc, will do

hpi.text: knee pain for 2 weeks, stiff in morning, swelling, increased activity
hpi.evidence_span: terrible knee pain that's been going on for about two weeks

pmh.evidence_span: just a mild sprain years ago

meds.evidence_span: i have hypertension so i'm on amlodipine

exam.text: right knee painful on weight bearing, effusion present, warm to touch, limited ROM
exam.evidence_span: bearing weight is painful and there is some effusion

problems.name: meniscal irritation or early osteoarthritis flare
problems.evidence_span: sounds like a meniscal irritation or early osteoarthritis flare

items.evidence_span: let's get an ultrasound to be sure
i'll write you a script for meloxicam
refer you to physio for strengthening
come back if the swelling gets worse

transcript:
HCP: hi, whats going on?
Patient: uh hi doctor, i'm just really feeling down and tired lately
HCP: that's rough, how long has this been going on
Patient: probably about two weeks now, i used to love going to the gym but i can't even get out of bed really
HCP: any weight loss or appetite changes?
Patient: yeah, haven't been eating much and lost like a stone
HCP: any specific medical history?
Patient: uh yeah, i have type 2 diabetes and high blood pressure
HCP: any meds you're on for those
Patient: metformin and amlodipine
HCP: any food or drug allergies?
Patient: uh no, nothing really
HCP: right, let me check your vitals, BP is 138 over 88, sounds a bit elevated for you
HCP: listening to your heart, sounds okay, lungs clear
HCP: abdomen soft, no tenderness
HCP: lower limbs feel for oedema
Patient: yeah, my ankles are a bit swollen
HCP: okay, so we've got the fatigue, weight loss, and the blood pressure running a bit high, sounds like we need to manage your diabetes and BP better
HCP: i'm going to adjust your meds and get a referral to the dietitian
HCP: also, if you get chest pain or trouble breathing, come back immediately
HCP: does that make sense?
Patient: yeah, makes sense, thanks doc

chief_complaint.evidence_span: really feeling down and tired lately

hpi.text: symptoms for 2 weeks, decreased appetite, weight loss
hpi.evidence_span: probably about two weeks now, i used to love going to the gym but i can't even get out of bed really

exam.text: BP 138/88, heart sounds okay, lungs clear, abdomen soft, ankle oedema
exam.evidence_span:
BP is 138 over 88, sounds a bit elevated for you
HCP: listening to your heart, sounds okay, lungs clear
HCP: abdomen soft, no tenderness
HCP: lower limbs feel for oedema
Patient: yeah, my ankles are a bit swollen

HCP: come back in a few days

transcript:
HCP: hello there, what seems to be the trouble?
Patient: hi doc, i've got this itchy rash on my arms that's been going on for a couple of weeks now
HCP: where exactly on your arms?
Patient: mostly the elbows and forearms, kind of bumpy and red
HCP: any itching at night?
Patient: yeah, keeps me awake sometimes
HCP: have you tried any creams?
Patient: just used some hydrocortisone from the chemist but it didn't work
HCP: any other meds or allergies i should know about?
Patient: i take a bit of ventolin for my asthma and i'm allergic to nuts
HCP: right, let me have a quick look
HCP: looks like eczema flare, keep the area moisturised and avoid triggers
HCP: also try a stronger steroid cream for a week
HCP: and if it's really itchy, some antihistamine might help
HCP: come back if it doesn't settle in another week

transcript:
HCP: hi there, how can i help?
Patient: hey doctor, my knee's been killing me for a couple weeks
HCP: where exactly?
Patient: the front of it, right below the kneecap
HCP: what does it feel like?
Patient: stiff in the morning, hurts when i go down stairs
HCP: any swelling?
Patient: a bit, it feels puffy
HCP: have you had any sports injuries recently?
Patient: no, nothing like that
HCP: any medical history?
Patient: just high blood pressure
HCP: meds?
Patient: taking amiodipine
HCP: allergies?
Patient: none that i know of
HCP: ok, let me check your knee
HCP: looks like some effusion, gentle pressure hurts
HCP: sounds like patellofemoral pain syndrome
HCP: i'll give you a steroid injection to calm it down
HCP: ice it twice a day and take ibuprofen if you can
HCP: physio once a week to strengthen the quads
HCP: come back in 6 weeks if it hasn't improved

hpi.text: pain for couple weeks, front of knee, stiff morning, hurts going down stairs, feels puffy
hpi.evidence_span: my knee's been killing me for a couple weeks

exam.text: effusion, pressure hurts

transcript:
HCP: hi there, how's it going?
Patient: good, thanks. just wanted to get checked out on my knee and also my back hurts a bit
HCP: right, where is the knee pain exactly?
Patient: uh more on the inside, feels kind of swollen and warm to the touch
HCP: how long has this been going on?
Patient: like two weeks now, got worse after running yesterday
HCP: any history of knee problems?
Patient: yeah, had a meniscus tear years ago, had surgery
HCP: ok, and what about the back?
Patient: just lower back, stiff in the morning but loosens up later
HCP: any red flags like numbness or tingling?
Patient: no, just feels stiff
HCP: right, let me have a look at the knee
Patient: okay
HCP: looks a bit swollen, tenderness over the medial joint line, range of motion is restricted a bit
HCP: i'm going to prescribe some anti-inflammatories and refer you to physio
Patient: sounds good
HCP: also for the back, try heat packs and gentle stretching, avoid heavy lifting for now
Patient: thanks doc

chief_complaint.evidence_span: wanted to get checked out on my knee and also my back hurts a bit

hpi.text: knee pain 2 weeks, worse after running, back pain morning stiffness
hpi.evidence_span: like two weeks now, got worse after running yesterday

pmh.evidence_span: had a meniscus tear years ago, had surgery

exam.text: knee swollen, tenderness medial joint line, restricted ROM
exam.evidence_span: looks a bit swollen, tenderness over the medial joint line, range of motion is restricted a bit

problems.name: knee inflammation/swelling
problems.evidence_span: feels kind of swollen and warm to the touch

problems.name: mechanical lower back pain
problems.evidence_span: just lower back, stiff in the morning but loosens up later

transcript:
HCP: hello there, how are you feeling today?
Patient: hi doc, just a bit crummy actually
HCP: sounds like you're not well, what's the main problem
Patient: well my ankle's been throbbing for a couple of days now
HCP: where exactly on the ankle
Patient: right side, pretty swollen and hot to touch
HCP: any injury or fall?
Patient: not really, just been on the footie field a bit hard last week
HCP: okay so it's been there since then, any redness
Patient: yeah, looks a bit pink near the top
HCP: let me have a look then
HCP: hmm, it is quite erythematous and tender to palpation
HCP: you've been limping around?
Patient: yeah, can't really put full weight on it
HCP: got any diabetes or circulation issues?
Patient: nah, pretty healthy otherwise
HCP: good, what meds you taking
Patient: just panadol for headaches sometimes
HCP: any allergies?
Patient: no, never had a bad reaction
HCP: okay so it seems like a sprain or maybe a bit of cellulitis
HCP: I'll give you some ibuprofen and tell you to elevate it
HCP: ice pack three times a day, keep it high up
HCP: and if it gets really red or hot, come back in a day or two
Patient: right, thanks for the help doc
HCP: no worries, take care now

hpi.text: swollen and hot ankle, redness near top, limping, since footie field last week
hpi.evidence_span: pretty swollen and hot to touch

exam.text: right side swollen and hot, erythematous and tender to palpation
exam.evidence_span: right side, pretty swollen and hot to touch

problems.name: sprain or cellulitis
problems.evidence_span: seems like a sprain or maybe a bit of cellulitis

items.action: ice pack three times a day

if it gets really red or hot, come back

transcript:
HCP: hi there, whats going on?
Patient: oh hi doctor, yeah so i've been having this weird rash on my arms for a couple of days now
HCP: where exactly?
Patient: like right here and here, mostly on the inside of my elbows
HCP: and is it itchy?
Patient: yeah pretty bad, makes me want to scratch it all the time
HCP: any new soaps or detergents you started using recently?
Patient: no, i haven't changed anything at home
HCP: right, any history of eczema or asthma?
Patient: no never had any of that stuff
HCP: ok, let me have a look, yeah these look like hives or urticaria
HCP: probably an allergic reaction to something you ate or maybe a bug bite, try antihistamines
HCP: take some cetirizine, and if it's not better in two days come back
Patient: thanks doctor

transcript:
HCP: good morning, what seems to be the trouble today
Patient: hi doc, i've got this bad knee pain again, been limping for a week
HCP: where exactly is the pain
Patient: right knee, mainly when I'm walking or bending down
HCP: any swelling or warmth
Patient: a bit swollen, feels tight but not hot
HCP: okay, let's examine it, please sit on the exam couch
HCP: I see some swelling around the joint and tenderness over the patella
HCP: sounds like a flare of your osteoarthritis, I'll give you some hydrocortisone cream and advise on activity modification
Patient: thanks, will that help the swelling
HCP: the cream helps with pain and inflammation, keep weight off it for a couple of days, physio can sort out the strength later
